ai.title: AI-Powered Predictions — How Accurate Are We?
ai.subtitle: park.fan uses its own AI model trained on millions of real queue records to forecast crowd levels and wait times at theme parks worldwide. The model factors in weather forecasts, school holiday calendars, and known events — and retrains automatically as fresh data arrives.
ai.avgErrorDesc: MAE — how many minutes off our predictions are on average
ai.rmseDesc: RMSE — how bad the worst misses are, outliers weighted more
ai.mapeDesc: MAPE — percentage error relative to actual wait time
ai.totalPredictionsDesc: verified against real recorded wait times
ai.coverageDesc: of all predictions cross-checked with actual data
ai.r2ScoreDesc: R² — how well the model explains wait time variance
ai.modelAge: Model trained {days} days ago · automatically retrained on new data
ai.modelAgeHours: Model trained {time} hours ago · automatically retrained on new data
ai.howText: Every wait time forecast is built from three layers of data: historical queue records collected from parks over years, external signals like weather forecasts and school holiday calendars, and contextual factors such as special events and seasonal patterns. The model generates both hourly wait time forecasts for individual rides and daily crowd level ratings for full park visits — available up to 365 days ahead.
ai.improvingText: park.fan is a living platform, still in active development. Every day the system automatically compares its earlier predictions against the wait times actually recorded at the parks, feeding those comparisons back into the next training cycle. The accuracy metrics above reflect this real-world performance — not synthetic benchmarks. New parks and attractions are added regularly, and the model becomes more accurate as it accumulates more verified data for each destination.

about.title: What Is park.fan?
about.p1: park.fan was born from a frustration every theme park fan knows: standing in a 90-minute queue and wondering whether smarter planning could have avoided it. What started as a side project—"let me just track some wait times"—grew into a full platform now covering 151 parks, 5,253 attractions, and millions of queue data records processed every day. The goal has always been the same: take the guesswork out of planning a theme park visit.
about.p2: The AI behind park.fan analyzes historical crowd data, weather forecasts, school holiday calendars, and special events to predict crowd levels and wait times days or even weeks ahead. Instead of guessing, you can check whether next Saturday at Europa-Park Resort or Phantasialand is going to be a green day—short queues, relaxed atmosphere—or a red day where every popular ride has a 60-minute wait. The model is continuously trained on new data, so its predictions get sharper over time.
about.p3: park.fan currently tracks 151 theme parks and 5,253 individual attractions worldwide—from Walt Disney World, Disneyland Resort, Universal Orlando, and Universal Studios Japan to European parks like Europa-Park, Phantasialand, Efteling, Heide Park, and Toverland, plus parks across Asia, Australia, and South America. New parks are added regularly as the platform continues to grow.
about.p4: Search for your destination park using the search bar, or browse by continent, country, and city. On the park page you will find live wait times for every ride, restaurant, and show—updated every minute—alongside AI crowd predictions and a day-by-day crowd calendar. Green days signal short queues and smaller crowds; orange or red days mean higher attendance and longer waits.
about.p5: park.fan is a living project, constantly evolving with new features and parks. Save your favourite parks for quick access, use in-park navigation to plan the most efficient route through the park, and always check the crowd calendar before booking to make sure your visit lands on the best possible day.

footer.description: Live wait times and AI-powered crowd predictions for 142+ theme parks worldwide. Plan your perfect park visit with real-time data and in-park navigation.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.waitTimesQ: What are the wait times at {park} today?
faq.waitTimesA: Right now the average wait at {park} is about {avg} minutes (peak {peak} minutes) across {operating} open attractions. Live wait times for every ride are shown above on this page.
faq.waitTimesNoDataA: Wait times at {park} vary by day, season and crowd level. Check the live wait times for every ride above, plus the crowd calendar to find the best time to visit.
faq.openingHoursQ: What are the opening hours for {park} today?
faq.openingHoursEvergreenA: Opening hours at {park} vary by season and day. Check the schedule and crowd calendar on this page for the current opening times.
faq.diningQ: What dining options are available at {park}?
faq.crowdCalendarA: Yes! park.fan provides a detailed crowd calendar for {park} showing predicted crowd levels for the next 30 days, helping you choose the best days to visit with shorter wait times.
faq.leastCrowdedA: {park} is typically quietest on {days}. Check the <calendar>crowd calendar</calendar> above for upcoming low-crowd days and plan your visit accordingly.
faq.leastCrowdedNoDataA: Check the <calendar>crowd calendar</calendar> on this page for upcoming quiet days. Green days signal short queues and smaller crowds.

attraction.title: Frequently Asked Questions
attraction.waitTimeQ: How long is the wait for {attraction}?
attraction.waitTimeA: Wait times for {attraction} vary by day and season. Check live wait times on park.fan.
attraction.waitTimeStatsA: Today the wait for {attraction} averages {avg} minutes, ranging from {min} to {max} minutes. The current live wait time is shown above on this page.
attraction.singleRiderQ: Does {attraction} have a Single Rider line?
attraction.singleRiderA: Yes, {attraction} has a Single Rider line for individual guests to fill empty seats.
attraction.paidQueueQ: Is there a fast pass options for {attraction}?
attraction.paidQueueA: Yes, expedited access is available via {type}.

faq.whatIsA: park.fan is your central platform for live wait times and current information from over 30 theme parks in Europe, Asia, and North America. We show you in real-time how long the wait times are at your favorite attractions, which shows are currently running, and when the parks are open.
faq.whichParksQ: Which theme parks are supported by park.fan?
faq.whichParksA: park.fan supports over 30 theme parks worldwide, including Phantasialand, Europa-Park, Disneyland Paris, Efteling, Walibi Belgium, Toverland, Bobbejaanland, and many more. You can find the complete list of all parks with live wait times on our parks overview page. We are continuously expanding our offering with additional theme parks.
faq.liveDataQ: Are the wait times on park.fan live and in real-time?
faq.liveDataA: Yes! park.fan continuously updates all wait times during park operating hours. The data comes directly from the official park APIs and is synchronized in real-time. You always see the most current available wait times and can optimally plan your park visit.
faq.freeA: Yes, park.fan is completely free to use. You can view all live wait times, opening hours, shows, and restaurants without registration or payment. Our goal is to provide the best possible service to every theme park visitor.
faq.favoritesQ: Can I save my favorite parks and attractions on park.fan?
faq.favoritesA: Yes! With park.fan's favorites feature, you can mark your favorite parks and favorite attractions. These will then be displayed on your personalized homepage, so you can immediately see the current wait times for your favorites.
faq.featuresQ: What information does park.fan offer besides live wait times?
faq.featuresA: In addition to live wait times for attractions, park.fan also provides park opening hours, show times, restaurant information, weather forecasts, historical wait time statistics, and crowd forecasts. This way, you can optimally plan your theme park visit and find the best times for your favorite attractions.
faq.mobileA: Absolutely! park.fan is fully responsive and works perfectly on smartphones and tablets. You can check live wait times directly in the theme park to spontaneously decide which attraction to visit next. The mobile version offers all features of the desktop version.

form.creditPlaceholder: How you want to be credited
form.consentLabel: I took these photos (or hold the rights) and grant park.fan a licence to publish them (after review), crediting me where given. I keep my copyright.
form.consentHint: Uploading is only possible once you tick this box.
form.requirements: Add at least one photo, pick a ride or park, accept the terms and pass the check.

rights.title: Your photos, your rights
rights.intro: Before you upload, here is exactly what happens with your pictures:
rights.ownership: You keep the copyright. You never sign your rights away — you simply grant us permission to use the photo.
rights.license: You grant park.fan a non-exclusive, worldwide, royalty-free licence to show, resize and optimise the photo on park.fan and our channels, with a credit where you provided one.
rights.moderation: Every upload is reviewed by a person before it appears. Nothing goes live automatically.
rights.ownContent: Only upload photos you took yourself or clearly hold the rights to — no other people who object to being shown, no material owned by someone else.
rights.withdraw: Changed your mind? Contact us any time and we will remove your photo.

success.title: Thank you!
success.message: {count, plural, one {Your photo has} other {Your # photos have}} been submitted and will appear in the gallery after a quick review.
